name: The St. Regis Bora Bora Resort

content.verdict: The St. Regis Bora Bora Resort is the most overtly opulent property on the island — the kind of place where a dedicated butler unpacks your luggage, arranges every detail without being asked, and learns your preferences within 24 hours. On Motu Ome'e, with the resort positioned to face Mount Otemanu on the eastern horizon, the overwater villa views are genuinely dramatic. The 4.8 TripAdvisor rating and 58% couples review percentage are the highest combination of any full-service Bora Bora property. Book here if your honeymoon budget has a ceiling and your standards don't — the butler-serviced villas, the overwater Iridium Spa, and the signature Bloody Mary service (a St. Regis ritual) create a total experience that goes beyond simply booking a nice bungalow. Skip it if butler service feels performative rather than genuinely helpful — the St. Regis model depends on you engaging with the service culture. Also: no pool, which matters if you want freshwater swimming. For couples for whom this is a truly once-in-a-lifetime experience with no budget ceiling, the St. Regis Bora Bora delivers.
content.best_room: Book the Overwater Villa — the base villa category at St. Regis is already exceptional, with a private plunge pool, indoor-outdoor shower, king bed facing the lagoon, and direct water steps. But for your honeymoon, request an upgrade to the Premier Overwater Villa which adds a larger deck, a wraparound plunge pool, and an outdoor daybed. Request a villa facing northeast for the most direct Mount Otemanu sightline. The Royal Estate and Owners Villa are extraordinary but at $10,000+ per night represent a different category entirely.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Butler Arrival & First Night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private speedboat from Bora Bora Airport meets you pier-side; the St. Regis butler is waiting at the dock. The 15-minute crossing to Motu Ome'e is your introduction to the St. Regis pace. Your butler will have already stocked your villa with your preferred drinks, arranged your preferred pillow menu, and set up the plunge pool at the ideal temperature. This evening: the signature St. Regis Bloody Mary at sunset, then dinner at Lagoon Restaurant by Jean-Georges — the finest dining on the island.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dawn Plunge & Iridium Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: The overwater Iridium Spa is among the most beautiful spa facilities in French Polynesia — glass walls over the lagoon, treatment tables suspended above the water, and a menu of Polynesian rituals that take the monoi experience to its highest expression. Book the 'Taureva Nui' couples treatment for 10am — a 2-hour ritual combining warm coconut shell massage and monoi body wrap. Your butler arranges the booking. Morning: snorkel from your villa steps; the reef here has hawksbill turtles most mornings.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lagoon Excursion & Shark Ray Ballet
itinerary_7_nights.description: The St. Regis runs its lagoon excursions in a beautifully maintained traditional va'a outrigger. The circuit includes the shark-and-ray feeding site — one of Bora Bora's most photographed experiences — and a snorkel stop at a pristine coral garden near the barrier reef. Return by noon. Your butler arranges lunch on your villa deck: the full room service menu brought to your plunge pool side, served on proper china with linen napkins.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Motu Tapu & Private Island Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge arranges a full-day private charter to Motu Tapu — the St. Regis version includes a butler accompanying you for the morning, a full picnic set-up with champagne, fresh fruit, and Polynesian food, plus premium snorkel equipment and a photographer for the first hour. The uninhabited motu has flawless white sand and the clearest water in the lagoon. Return by 3pm. Evening: cocktails on your villa deck as the sun sets behind Otemanu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Jean-Georges Dinner & Dive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning: the resort's PADI dive center runs two-tank dives to Anau manta ray site — one of the world's most famous dive experiences. Even beginners can do the Discover Scuba version. Return for a late lunch. This evening: the Jean-Georges Vongerichten restaurant at the St. Regis is the best fine-dining experience in Bora Bora — a French-inflected Polynesian tasting menu with a wine cellar to match. Book the Chef's Table experience for a private dining room adjacent to the kitchen.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunset Sail & Overwater Private Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: The St. Regis' private catamaran sunset sail is exclusive to guests: small group, premium champagne, and the definitive circuit of the inner lagoon with Mount Otemanu views from every angle. Return at dusk. Tonight your butler arranges the private overwater dinner: a dedicated pier decorated with thousands of tiare petals and floating candles, a six-course Polynesian menu, a Tahitian guitar duo playing softly from a neighboring platform. This is the dinner you'll describe for the rest of your lives.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Ritual & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: The St. Regis offers a late checkout of 2pm for honeymooners on request. Use the morning for a final plunge pool session, a slow in-villa breakfast that your butler arranges without you needing to ask, and a last snorkel from your steps. The butler packs your luggage while you're in the water and has it loaded on the transfer boat. The private speedboat back to the airport takes 15 minutes. The St. Regis farewell is precisely managed — you'll feel cared for until the moment you board your plane.

honest_caveats: No swimming pool — the plunge pool in each villa is private but shallow and small. Couples who want a proper infinity pool or freshwater swimming will miss it; the lagoon is the only option for real swimming.

The St. Regis is among the most expensive resorts in French Polynesia. Premier Overwater Villas run $1,500–$2,500 per night in peak season — a 7-night honeymoon in this category alone can exceed $15,000 before food, transfers, or activities.

The butler service, while excellent, requires couples to be comfortable being cared for. Some honeymooners find having a butler intrusive or feel pressure to interact with the service culture throughout their stay.

The motu location and the self-contained luxury bubble means you can easily spend 7 nights without meaningful interaction with local Polynesian culture. Make deliberate plans to visit Vaitape or a vanilla plantation for cultural depth.
content.hotel_email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon Villa Reservation – [Your Names] – [Check-in Date]

Dear St. Regis Bora Bora Butler Services Team,

My partner and I are honeymooning at The St. Regis Bora Bora Resort from [check-in date] to [check-out date] (Marriott Bonvoy # [XXXXXXX], Booking reference: [XXXXXX]). This is our honeymoon and it represents a very special trip for us.

We would love your help in advance of arrival:

1. We have booked a Premier Overwater Villa and would love a villa with a direct northeast-facing view of Mount Otemanu — as far from neighboring villas as possible for privacy.
2. For our sixth evening ([date]), please could you arrange the private overwater dinner experience — the full decorated pier setup with tiare petals, candlelight, and live Tahitian guitar?
3. Please note our beverage preferences for the welcome stock: [champagne/wine/spirits preferences], and [mineral water still/sparkling] in abundance.

We trust your team completely — anything you feel would make our honeymoon more special is very welcome.

With warmth,
[Your Names]

faqs.question: Is The St. Regis Bora Bora worth it for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: If budget is not a constraint, yes — it's the most complete luxury honeymoon experience in Bora Bora. The butler service, Jean-Georges dining, Iridium Spa, and Premier Overwater Villas combine into a package that no other property on the island fully replicates. The lack of a swimming pool and the very high cost are the only meaningful trade-offs.

faqs.question: What overwater bungalow category should we book?
faqs.answer: Book the Premier Overwater Villa — the wraparound plunge pool and expanded deck justify the premium over base villas for a honeymoon. Request a northeast-facing position for direct Mount Otemanu views. Avoid villas directly adjacent to the main pier walkway — the outer arm positions are noticeably more private and quieter.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October offer the best combination of weather, calm lagoon, and lower rates. July–August is peak season: the St. Regis is fully booked and rates are at their highest. Even at this five-star property, shoulder season rates can be 25–35% lower than peak — a meaningful saving at these price levels.

faqs.question: How do you get to The St. Regis Bora Bora Resort?
faqs.answer: Fly Papeete to Bora Bora Airport on Air Tahiti (50 minutes). The St. Regis private speedboat transfer meets you at the airport pier — 15 minutes to Motu Ome'e. Your butler is waiting at the dock. For the most memorable arrival, book the Air Tahiti Nui seaplane: it lands directly at the resort's water runway — approximately $400 per person each way.

faqs.question: What does the honeymoon package include?
faqs.answer: The St. Regis romance package typically includes a floral villa decoration, champagne and canapes on arrival, daily breakfast, one Iridium Spa couples treatment, and the private overwater dinner setup. Marriott Bonvoy Titanium/Ambassador members receive suite upgrades and dining credits. Confirm current inclusions at booking — the full package is the best value if it includes the spa and private dinner.

faqs.question: One insider tip for honeymooners at The St. Regis Bora Bora?
faqs.answer: Tell your butler on the first morning that you want the private overwater dinner experience arranged for night six, and that you'd love a Tahitian guitarist accompanying it. Then don't think about logistics for the rest of the trip. That's the St. Regis model — hand everything to the butler once and let the team handle the details. The couples who enjoy it most are those who genuinely surrender the planning.

true_cost_breakdown.item: Speedboat or seaplane transfer (per couple, round trip)
